title: Math — Linear Inequalities
description: SAT-style linear inequality questions in one and two variables, including compound inequalities, systems, and modeling constraints.

questions.prompt: The length of a rectangle is 50 inches and the width is \(z\) inches. The perimeter is at most 210 inches. Which inequality represents this situation?

A model estimates that whales from the genus \(\textit{Eschrichtius}\) travel 72 to 77 miles each day during their migration. Which inequality represents the estimated total number of miles \(s\) a whale could travel in 16 days?
questions.explanation: Daily distance is between 72 and 77 miles. Over 16 days, multiply both bounds by 16: \(72\cdot16\le s\le77\cdot16\).

questions.prompt: The inequalities are \(y > 2x - 1\) and \(2x > 5\). Which of the following consists of the \(y\)-coordinates of all points that satisfy the system?
questions.explanation: From \(2x>5\), \(x>\frac{5}{2}\). Then \(y>2x-1>2\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)-1=4\). So all solutions have \(y>4\).

questions.prompt: A triangle has side lengths of 6 and 12. Which inequality represents the possible lengths \(z\) of the third side?

A moving truck can tow a trailer if the combined weight of the trailer and the boxes it contains is no more than 4,600 pounds. If the trailer weighs 500 pounds and each box weighs 120 pounds, what is the maximum number of boxes the truck can tow?
questions.explanation: Model: \(500+120b\le4600\Rightarrow120b\le4100\Rightarrow b\le34.16\ldots\). Max whole number is 34.

questions.prompt: An elephant weighs 200 pounds at birth and gains more than 2 but less than 3 pounds per day during its first year. Which inequality represents all possible weights \(w\) (in pounds) 365 days after birth?
The inequalities are \(y > 14\) and \(4x + y < 18\). The point \((x, 53)\) is a solution to the system. Which of the following could be the value of \(x\)?
questions.explanation: With \(y=53\), \(53>14\) is true. Also \(4x+53<18\Rightarrow4x<-35\Rightarrow x<-8.75\). Only −9 works.

questions.prompt: Marisa needs to hire at least 10 staff members. She will pay $640 per week to each junior director and $880 per week to each senior director. Her budget is no more than $9,700 per week. She must hire at least 3 junior directors and at least 1 senior director. Which system of inequalities represents the conditions, if \(x\) is the number of junior directors and \(y\) is the number of senior directors?
questions.explanation: Budget “no more than” gives \(640x+880y\le9700\). At least 10 staff: \(x+y\ge10\). At least 3 junior: \(x\ge3\). At least 1 senior: \(y\ge1\).

questions.prompt: A home’s average annual energy cost is $4,334. A geothermal system costs $25,000 to install and reduces the annual energy cost to $2,712. Which inequality can be solved to find \(t\), the number of years after installation at which total energy cost savings will exceed the installation cost?
Given \(y < -4x + 4\), which point \((x,y)\) is a solution?
questions.explanation: Test \((-4,0)\): RHS is \(-4(-4)+4=20\). Since \(0<20\), it satisfies the inequality. The other points do not.
